original_article:
SYDNEY (Reuters) — A major cyberattack on Australia's telecommunications infrastructure has crippled communications networks across the country's eastern seaboard, affecting operations at major corporations including BHP Group Ltd.

BHP, the world's largest mining company by market capitalisation, confirmed that its Melbourne headquarters and several operational sites in Western Australia experienced significant communications outages after the attack disrupted Telstra and Optus networks on Sunday. The Metals & Mining giant said it had activated emergency satellite communications but warned of delays in logistics coordination and export documentation.

"Our mining operations continue to run, but our ability to coordinate shipments and communicate with global customers has been materially impaired," a BHP spokesperson said.

The Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) attributed the attack to a sophisticated state-sponsored actor and said restoration of full telecommunications services could take seven to ten days. The incident has affected banking, logistics, and resource sector operations nationwide.

Australia's telecommunications sector has faced increased cybersecurity threats in recent months, with the Optus data breach and Medibank hack highlighting vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure.

noisy_versions.mild:
SYDNEY (Reuters) — A major cyberattack on Australia's telecommunications infrastructure has crippled communications networks across the country's eastern seaboard, affecting operations at major corporations including BHP Group Ltd. The global cybersecurity industry has seen a sharp increase in sophisticated attacks targeting critical infrastructure over the past year.

BHP, the world's largest mining company by market capitalisation, confirmed that its Melbourne headquarters and several operational sites in Western Australia experienced significant communications outages after the attack disrupted Telstra and Optus networks on Sunday. The Metals & Mining giant said it had activated emergency satellite communications but warned of delays in logistics coordination and export documentation.

The Australian Signals Directorate attributed the attack to a sophisticated state-sponsored actor and said restoration of full telecommunications services could take seven to ten days. In separate technology news, Australia's government announced increased funding for quantum computing research. The incident has affected banking, logistics, and resource sector operations nationwide.
noisy_versions.moderate:
Cybersecurity threats to critical infrastructure have escalated globally, with governments and corporations investing heavily in defensive capabilities. Australia has been a frequent target, with several high-profile incidents in recent years.

A sophisticated cyberattack targeting Telstra and Optus networks has knocked out telecommunications across eastern Australia. BHP Group Ltd, the world's largest mining company, confirmed severe outages at its Melbourne headquarters and Western Australian operations. The Metals & Mining company deployed satellite communications but warned of material delays in logistics and customer coordination.

The Australian Signals Directorate attributed the attack to a state-sponsored actor, estimating seven to ten days for full restoration. Some cybersecurity analysts have drawn comparisons to the Colonial Pipeline attack in the US. Banking, logistics, and resource operations across Australia have been affected. The Australian government has convened an emergency cabinet meeting on critical infrastructure protection.
noisy_versions.high:
Technology stocks led global markets higher on Tuesday, with the NASDAQ composite gaining 1.8% on strong earnings from major cloud computing providers. Apple announced a new AI partnership with Google, sending both stocks to record highs.

In Australia, a major cyberattack has crippled telecommunications networks, affecting corporations including BHP Group Ltd. The Metals & Mining giant's Melbourne headquarters and Western Australian operations experienced severe outages. BHP activated satellite communications but warned of significant logistics delays. The Australian Signals Directorate attributed the attack to a state-sponsored actor and estimated restoration could take seven to ten days.

In other cybersecurity news, the European Union adopted its new Cyber Resilience Act. A ransomware group claimed responsibility for attacks on three US hospital systems. Japan's cybersecurity agency reported a 40% increase in attacks on industrial control systems. Meanwhile, global semiconductor sales rose 15% year-over-year, driven by AI chip demand. SpaceX successfully launched its latest Starlink satellite batch.
